body: Do we need to codify "internal version" further?  Why not something like a "version set" or just group?
Why not use `json` tags - yaml parser still respects them, I think?
I am confused - in the first commit you build scaffolding around YAML files ("apiversion.yaml") to indicate API metadata, but then you never use it?  No such file in this PR?
This construction feels tautological -- can you add comments on what's going on in things like this?
what if there's an `=true` and then an `=false` -- should that be an error or should the last one win?
this one does not spec `staging/src` but the next one does - on purpose?

I still find it a little confusing to have 2 dirs which each specify overlapping parts of the same object (e.g. the example in staging/src/k8s.io/api/apps/apigroup.yaml and pkg/apis/apps/apigroup.yaml

Would it make sense to put all of the yaml into one file and xref the internal and external dirs?

Maybe let me ask a different question.  Is it s goal that someone be able to `git clone` the standalone k8s.io/api repo and run (some of) the codegen against it, and have it properly update the parts that are stored in that repo?  E.g. it would work for client-gen but not validation-gen.

If yes, then we NEED split yaml.  If no, then maybe we don't?
